rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The trustee's sixth (and final) account and report not filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARINA NIKOL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/23/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Did Marta Eisenberg consent in writing to receive electronic notice? [CRC 2.251.] (2) The only correct form of notice in Conservatorship cases is the mandatory Judicial Council form GC-020, to which the instructions for how to appear remotely must be attached. If a pleading form of proof of service is used, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. (3) The pleading form of proof of electronic service filed on 8/13/24 (without a copy of the Notice of Hearing attached to it) shows that notice was served on 8/13/24, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (4) A Court-appointed fiduciary must supplement or amend his own pleading(s). [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (5) The "Second Supplemental Declaration" filed on 8/13/24 AMENDED the petition, so the caption was misleading, and it still did not fully address all of the issues noted in the tentative ruling for 6/3/24: a. Paragraph 7 of the petition states that the aggregate value of the estate is $1,486,953.22, but paragraph 9 shows the starting total as $1,470,549.33. b. That summary of account in paragraph 9 of the petition does not balance. c. Where on the informational schedule is the acquisition of the GE Healthcare shares shown? (6) Ending balances shown in the account must be reconciled to the statements. [LRSF 14.35 D.] The Wells Fargo checking account statement ending 4/25/23 shows an amount that differs from the amount shown in the accounting. (The Wells Fargo Advisors Acct xx9770 also has some nominal cash, which should be listed under cash.) (7) There is also no reconciliation showing the difference between the Wells Fargo account xxx6790 balance reported in the I&As and shown on the statement for the period ending 4/7/22. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/14/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and DIVIDED FROM THE REST OF THE DOCUMENT PAGE BY A PRINTED LINE. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZOILA REGINA JEREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/28/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proposed distribution appears to be incorrect. Net distributable cash should be $671,509.28. (2) The attorney costs paid on 4/18/24 ($1,518.40) must be itemized (date, payee, description, amount). (3) Reimbursements to an individual, in particular, the fiduciary, must show who the original payee was (locksmith, heir finder, eviction attorney, etc.). (4) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (5) Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. Although file stamped on 6/18/24, the amended petition was not actually e-filed until 6/24/24, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (6) Notice of remote appearance is not required for Probate hearings and is NOT the notice to interested parties of how they may appear remotely. Furthermore, the combined Notice of Remote Appearance and Access filed on 6/21/24 was not properly verified, and the proof of service attached to it (?) was proof of mailing the Notice of Remote Appearance and Access, not the Notice of Hearing of the amended petition. (A petitioner does not need to give notice to himself.) (7) The initial petition for letters indicated that Decedent's two predeceased children had "no issue surviving," which is not the same as their not having any issue, ever. Did Rosa Jerez or Carlos Velasquez, Jr., have any issue and, if so, when did they die? If there were any such issue and they post-deceased, they or their successor(s)-in-interest were entitled to notice at the outset. (The administrator, as the proposed personal representative named in item 2 of the Petition for Letters of Administration, was also missing from item 8 of that petition.) Please provide a new proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/18/24. An AMENDMENT to the amended petition must be filed to correct the (lower) proposed distribution to each heir. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MANUEL MINERO AND VINCENTA MINERO LIVING TRUST, AS AMENDED MARCH 1, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: *REPOSTED 8/22/2024 at 2:57 p.m.* Continued to 10/14/24 per the judicial officer after reviewing the case file. The following issues must be cleared two weeks prior to the next hearing: 1. Need further allegation of how the court can approve the proposed distribution presented pursuant to the 2007 Amendment. The original 2005 Trust, Article Seventh, the fourth paragraph expressly limits the (surviving) grantor's right to amend the trust as to his/her separate property only. a) The petition is silent about the characterization of the trust assets. Page 2 "Status and Character of the Estate" only reports the status, not the character of the trust assets. b) The petition does not allege all interested persons have agreed to the request (regardless of the character), either. 2. Notice of Hearing (DE-120) with a copy of the petition must be given 30-days prior to the hearing. Prob. Code sec.17203. Proof of service on file or a notice of continued hearing does not meet the statutory notice requirement. No service to the temp trustee at all. 3. Proof of Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access not given. LRSF 4.1.C.6), either. 4. Petition to set forth Brea Steele's date of birth and to identify the guardian of the estate of the minor. The identified guardian of the estate must be properly noticed. 5. Need further allegation regarding the request for "statutory" compensation the attorney and the temp trustee. Petition seeks the court to grant "statutory" compensation to the fiduciary and the attorney in the caption and the prayer; however, this is not a petition for fee as the petition body itself does not make any relevant allegation or proof. It even alleges that the trustee has been paid. The petition is brought under Prob. Code sec. 17200, and this is not a decedent's estate case. Prob. Code sec. 17200 does not warrant statutory fees. Fees may be determined by the terms of the trust itself in general. 6. No proposed order submitted to the court and served to the objecting party. LRSF 4.14.7.A.

The court advised the counsel to file a document for the allegation made at the hearing on 3/6/24.

[CRC 7.51(b).] (4) No summons has been issued or filed. [Probate Code Section 8271.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/18/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
